summary: runtimekit ships an obfuscated Function()-based dropper that executes on require/import
details: Both the CommonJS entrypoint lib/index.cjs and the ESM entrypoint lib/index.mjs contain a top-level IIFE that uses a custom deterministic string-shuffler `YWG` to decode two ~1.5KB opaque string literals into (a) the body of a dynamically-constructed `Function` and (b) its argument data, then immediately invokes the constructed function: `Function('', Function('', YWG(<body>))(YWG(<data>)))(7942)`. Before doing so, the code deliberately exposes `global.r = require` and `global.m = module` so that the dynamically-built function — which has no lexical closure to its caller — can resolve arbitrary modules and access the module object. After execution it sets the marker `global._V = "A6-Shadow-16"`. The duplication across CJS and ESM ensures the payload fires regardless of how a consumer loads the package; any project that `require`s or `import`s runtimekit executes attacker-controlled code on the installer's machine. The obfuscation has no legitimate purpose for the package's stated functionality and exists to evade review.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
